description: A developmental psychology professor is analyzing data from 30 children assessed with the computerized Matching Familiar Figures Test (MFFT) for a study on cognitive styles. Clean the trial data from ~/pebl/data/mfft_data.csv by excluding child-99, who exhibits a mechanical button-mashing artifact (mean RTs < 150ms). For the remaining participants, calculate the mean reaction time (mean_first_rt_ms) and total errors across 12 trials, then determine the sample medians for these metrics. Classify each valid participant's cognitive style using Kagan's median-split logic: Reflective (mean_first_rt_ms >= median AND total_errors < median), Impulsive (mean_first_rt_ms < median
